entries.problem: Wire Feed Motor Runs but Wire Does Not Feed Properly
causes: Insufficient wire feed pressure
Incorrect wire feed roller size
Damaged MIG Gun, cable, or liner assembly
Feed Tensioner is too tight
solutions: Increase wire feed pressure properly. Follow step 27 on page 17.
Flip roll to correct size. Follow the Feed Roller instructions on page 12.
Have a qualified technician inspect these parts and replace as necessary.
Loosen Feed Tensioner so it applies only enough pressure to prevent continued spinning after the Gun Trigger is released.

entries.problem: Wire Creates a Bird's Nest During Operation
causes: Excess wire feed pressure
Incorrect Contact Tip size
MIG Gun Cable Connector not fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism
Damaged liner
solutions: Adjust wire feed pressure properly. Follow step 27 on page 17.
Replace with the proper tip for wire used.
Insert Gun Cable Connector properly. Follow steps 13 and 14 on page 13.
Have a qualified technician inspect and repair or replace as necessary.

causes: Gun cable is severely bent and Wire Feed mechanism cannot feed wire
Gun liner is clogged or worn
Gun liner is too small for welding wire being used
Wire is tangled on the spool
Wire is not making contact with Feed Rollers
Feed Roller is not making enough contact with wire or is crushing flux-cored wire
solutions: Straighten Gun cable.
Check gun liner for obstruction. Replace if necessary.
Check that gun liner is correct size for wire.
Check wire for cross winding or tangled spool.
Check Feed Rollers and ensure correct groove for wire diameter is being used.
Check Feed Tensioner and ensure it is set properly.

causes: Wire not feeding properly
Incorrect Contact Tip or liner size or excessive wear
Incorrect wire feed speed
Loose MIG Gun cable or ground cable
Damaged MIG Gun or loose connection within Gun
Incorrect polarity for process being run
Gas coverage may be insufficient or too high
Poor connection with workpiece
solutions: See first Troubleshooting section above.
Replace with the proper tip or liner size for wire used.
Adjust wire feed speed to achieve a more stable arc.
Check to ensure that all connections are tight.
Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.
Ensure polarity is correct for operation: DCEP for MIG welding and DCEN for Flux-Cored self-shielded welding.
Ensure gas flow rate is set according to Settings Chart. Make sure MIG Gun Cable Connector is fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism with no O-Rings exposed.
Check the ground clamp connection to the workpiece and machine. Ensure the MIG Gun is properly secured.

causes: Incorrect line voltage
Improper gauge or length of cord
Not enough current
solutions: Check the line voltage and, if insufficient, have a licensed electrician remedy the situation.
Do not use an extension cord on this Welder. Use only one of the supplied power cords for this Welder or an identical replacement cord.
Switch current to proper setting for metal thickness.

entries.problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On
causes: Tripped thermal protection device
Circuit supplies insufficient input voltage or amperage
Faulty or improperly connected Trigger
Machine is in low- or over-voltage protection
Machine is in the incorrect mode
solutions: If a warning screen appears in the LCD Display window, Welder may have overheated and shut down. Stop and wait with the Power Switch ON for the Welder to cool. Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods.
Verify that the circuit is designed to supply the required input voltage and amperage as detailed on the Specifications table. Check input voltage to ensure it is within specified range.
Ensure the gun connection is properly seated on machine. Qualified technician must check and secure/replace Trigger.
Check input voltage and ensure it falls within the specified range. If input voltage is correct, press Reset Button on back of machine.
Ensure the correct process has been selected.

entries.problem: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On
causes: Unit is not connected to outlet properly
Outlet is unpowered
Plug does not have correct rating
Circuit breaker has tripped due to high input amperage
Input Power Cord is not seated properly
solutions: Verify the voltage at the outlet and the connection to the outlet.
Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices; if any are tripped, determine and remedy cause before resetting.
Make sure installed plug is correct rating. See Specifications on page 7.
Press Reset Button on back of machine to reset circuit breaker.
Ensure the twist lock input Power Cord is fully secured.

entries.problem: Wire Feeds, but Arc Does Not Ignite
causes: Improper ground connection
Improperly sized Contact Tip
Excessively worn Contact Tip
Dirty Contact Tip
solutions: Make certain that the workpiece is contacted properly by the Ground Clamp and that the workpiece is properly cleaned near the ground clamp and welding location.
Verify that Contact Tip is the proper size for welding wire. If needed, replace Contact Tip with proper size and type.
Check that the hole in the tip is not deformed or enlarged. If needed, replace Contact Tip with proper size and type.
Properly clean Contact Tip.

causes: Shielding gas bottle is empty
Not enough or too much shielding gas
Dirty workpiece
Gun is being used too far away from workpiece
Polarity is incorrect for the application
Dirty welding wire is introducing contamination into the weld
solutions: Check gas bottle and replenish as necessary.
Check gas regulator to ensure proper flow.
Clean workpiece down to bare metal.
Check CTWD (contact tip to work distance) for the proper procedure.
Check the polarity and ensure it is DCEP for MIG and DCEN for Flux-Cored.
Make certain that welding wire is clean and free of rust and residues.

entries.problem: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On (TIG/Stick)
causes: Tripped thermal protection device
Faulty or improperly connected Trigger
Ground Clamp not attached to workpiece
Shielding Gas not connected
solutions: Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods to help reduce wear on the welder. Refer to Duty Cycle (Duration of Use) on page 29.
Qualified technician must check and secure/replace Trigger.
Attach Ground Clamp to workpiece.
Connect shielding gas to Welder.

entries.problem: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On (TIG/Stick)
causes: Unit is not connected to outlet properly
Outlet is unpowered
solutions: Verify the voltage at the outlet and the connection to the outlet.

Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices; if any are tripped, determine and remedy cause before resetting. Verify that the circuit is designed to supply the required input amperage as detailed in Specifications on page 7.

causes: Incorrect line voltage
Improper gauge or length of cord
solutions: Check the line voltage and, if insufficient, have a licensed electrician remedy the situation.
Do not use an extension cord on this Welder. Use only one of the supplied power cords for this Welder or an identical replacement cord.

causes: Loose electrode cable or ground cable
Damaged electrode holder or loose connection within electrode holder
Adjust current setting
Shielding gas getting low
solutions: Check to ensure that all connections are tight.
Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.
Make sure setting matches recommended setting on chart.
Replace shielding gas cylinder.
